title: NOC 51120 – Producers, Directors, Choreographers, and Related Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Producers, Directors, Choreographers, and Related Occupations under NOC 51120 are creative professionals responsible for planning, directing, and managing artistic productions across Canada’s film, television, broadcasting, music, gaming, and performing arts industries. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation usually requires a university degree or specialized artistic training. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5112 – Creative and performing artistsNOC Code51120

duties: Film, Radio, Television, and Video Game Producers
Plan, organize, and control production stages and schedules
Select and guide actors and technical crew
Coordinate with specialists during production and post-production
Create dance routines for film, theatre, and television
Oversee artistic design of sets, costumes, props, and visual elements
Edit and arrange film segments for continuity and thematic effect
Direct musicians and manage technical production processes
Determine visual composition, camera angles, and lighting effects
employment_requirements: University degree or college diploma in performing arts, broadcasting, journalism, theatre production, business administration, or film studies
Experience in production or technical roles
Degree or diploma in dance or formal training with a dance company
Degree or diploma in music or recording arts
Degree or diploma in film studies or cinematography
Experience in camera operation or related technical roles
salary_prospects: Producers, Directors, Choreographers, and Related Occupations typically earn:
$39,500 – $92,400 per year,depending on province, production scale, and specialization.
With 15,700 projected job openings and 13,300 job seekers, NOC 51120 reflects strong growth potential in Canada’s creative industries.
Growth of Canadian film and gaming industries
Rising demand for multimedia and entertainment content
Opportunities are strongest in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
NOC 51120 is classified under TEER 1, making it generally eligible under:
Eligibility depends on education credential assessment (ECA), language proficiency, and relevant work experience.

labour_market_demand: Growth of Canada’s film and streaming industries
Expansion of digital and video game production
With more job openings than job seekers projected, employment prospects are considered favorable.
Creative leadership roles in entertainment industries
Opportunities in film, television, gaming, and performing arts
Creative professionals under this NOC contribute significantly to Canada’s cultural and entertainment economy.
